seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
new_constraint: Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. Allocate time for team meetings. Do not allocate any time for team meetings in the schedule.
response:
Here is a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. **Note:** As per your specific instruction, **no time has been allocated for team meetings** in this schedule. The entire workday is dedicated to coding, testing, and individual collaboration. ### **Weekly Schedule Overview** | Day | Focus Area | Time Allocation (9:00 AM – 5:00 PM) | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **Monday** | **Sprint Planning & Architecture** | • 09:00 – 12:00: Deep work on technical design and API definitions.
• 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch.
• 13:00 – 17:00: Implementation of core logic and database schema. | | **Tuesday** | **Core Development** | • 09:00 – 12:00: Coding user interface components and backend integration.
• 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch.
• 13:00 – 17:00: Refactoring code and writing unit tests for new modules. | | **Wednesday** | **Integration & Debugging** | • 09:00 – 12:00: Connecting frontend and backend services; fixing integration bugs.
• 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch.
• 13:00 – 17:00: Performance optimization and resolving edge cases. | | **Thursday** | **Testing & QA** | • 09:00 – 12:00: Executing automated test suites and manual testing scenarios.
• 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch.
• 13:00 – 17:00: Bug fixing based on test results and code review feedback. | | **Friday** | **Polish & Documentation** | • 09:00 – 12:00: Final UI polish, error handling, and documentation updates.
• 12:00 – 13:00: Lunch.
• 13:00 – 17:00: Preparing deployment scripts and final code cleanup. | ### **Key Constraints Applied** * **No Meetings:** All standard stand-ups, daily syncs, and retrospectives have been removed from the schedule to maximize development time. * **Continuous Flow:** The schedule assumes a "flow state" environment where context switching is minimized. * **Lunch Breaks:** A standard 1-hour lunch break is included for rest, but no other breaks or administrative tasks are scheduled.
